preamble: The shift from a mid-front nasalized vowel [ẽ] to a mid-back rounded nasalized vowel [õ] involves rounding and backing, often influenced by adjacent labial or velar consonants.

examples.note: Backing and rounding of nasalized vowels in specific phonetic environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
